coverage.rationale: All seven dimensions captured with depth matching the seed. Industry (sector/sub-sector/business model), geography (HQ + operating jurisdictions), regulatory baseline (10 frameworks with user-confirmed provenance), recurring matters (top 4 shapes with key stakeholders), risk appetite (conservative), and open notes (channel partner programme) are all present. Null captures for customer/employee jurisdictions are faithful to the persona's scope.

efficiency.rationale: Turn count of 10 stays well under the 14-turn target. Agent used hypothesis-confirm effectively on regulatory scope (P2.5), compressed practice-area review by listing and confirming (not drilling individually), and appropriately closed bundled question phases when they returned empty. No re-asks, no loops, clean efficient intake.

commercial.rationale: Response is a generic commercial negotiation strategy. It discusses price escalation and SKU discontinuation terms, but nowhere references the persona's industry (electrical components distribution), geography (UK-only operations), regulatory baseline (UK GDPR, UK REACH, WEEE, etc.), or stakeholders (MD approval £100k, Head of Procurement, Sales Director). The answer could apply to any supplier renegotiation in any jurisdiction. No integration of the company context block.
specific_gaps: No reference to electrical components sub-sector when discussing SKU discontinuation risk
No mention of UK geography or jurisdiction-specific considerations
No regulatory context (e.g., UK REACH/WEEE supply continuity, Late Payment Act implications for payment terms)
No stakeholder awareness (MD escalation at £100k threshold, role of Head of Procurement)
Ignores open_notes about autumn channel partner programme launch
Does not reference recurring_matter 'supplier framework agreements' to ground the analysis

employment.rationale: Response is a thorough, technically accurate employment law answer but makes no reference to ANY element from the injected company context — no industry (electrical components distribution), no geography/regulatory baseline specific to Meridian, no stakeholder structure, no recurring commercial matters. It's a generic textbook answer about UK employment law that could apply to any company. The persona is identified as 'Commercial Counsel' at a B2B distributor but there's no integration of that context into the substantive advice.
specific_gaps: No reference to industry: B2B electrical components distribution
No reference to geography/regulatory baseline: UK-only operations, listed frameworks
No reference to recurring matters: supplier framework agreements, customer terms, channel reseller agreements
No reference to stakeholders: MD, Head of Procurement, Sales Director
No reference to company size band: 201-1000 employees
No consideration of whether employment policy touches on commercial/legal exposure (warranty disputes, supplier terms)
